channel.title: Selected Work on Alex Chen
channel.description: Recent content in Selected Work on Alex Chen

item.title: Design System Kit
item.description:
A lightweight design system starter that prioritizes pragmatism over completeness.

Philosophy

Most design systems fail because they’re too ambitious. This kit starts small: tokens, a few core components, and clear documentation. Teams can extend it as needed rather than fighting against an opinionated framework.

Features

  • CSS custom properties for theming
  • Accessible React components
  • Minimal dependencies
  • Dark mode support out of the box

item.title: Quiet Reader
item.description:
Quiet Reader is a reading app designed for focus. No social features. No sync conflicts. Just your books and articles, presented beautifully.

Problem

Most reading apps are cluttered with features you don’t need: social sharing, achievement badges, algorithmic recommendations. For people who just want to read, this noise is exhausting.

Solution

Quiet Reader strips away everything non-essential. The interface is deliberately minimal: a book cover, your reading position, and the text itself. Settings are limited to typography, spacing, and theme — the things that actually affect reading comfort.
